_readme.how_to_add: Add a new object to the 'schools' array below. Required fields: name, country, type, curriculum_usage, description. Optional: website, featured, badge.

field_definitions.name: Full legal name of the school (string, required)
field_definitions.description: One to two sentences describing the school and how it uses Altair (string, required)
field_definitions.featured: Set to true to highlight this school in the featured section (boolean, optional, default false)
field_definitions.badge: Either 'certified' or 'advanced'. Only set after official review (string, optional)

_readme.badge_request: To request a featured listing or badge upgrade, open an issue at github.com/altair-curriculum/registry with the subject line 'Badge Request: [School Name]' and include evidence of curriculum adoption.

schools.name: Greenfield Primary School
schools.description: Greenfield Primary has adopted the Altair K-12 curriculum across all year levels, with dedicated computing sessions three times per week. Their students have won regional coding competitions for three consecutive years.

schools.name: St. Catherine's Academy
schools.description: St. Catherine's integrates Altair lessons across Years 1 through 6, supplementing their existing ICT programme. The school has been recognised by the local authority for innovation in digital education.

schools.name: Riverside Community School
schools.description: Riverside uses selected Altair modules in their Grades 3 to 5 classes as an introduction to computational thinking. The school is piloting full adoption for the next academic year.

schools.name: Maplewood International School
schools.description: Maplewood runs the complete Altair K-12 programme from Kindergarten through Year 8, making it one of the first international schools in South-East Asia to achieve full Altair adoption.

schools.name: Harborview Elementary
schools.description: Harborview adopted the Altair K-2 and Grades 3-5 curriculum after a successful pilot in 2023. Computing is now embedded across literacy and maths lessons rather than taught as a separate subject.

schools.name: Thornton School of Excellence
schools.description: Thornton uses Altair materials in their senior computing elective and is expanding coverage to junior year groups from next term.
